By the end of the day, you'll have scrolled past 500 brands. Tomorrow, you'll remember none. No, your feed isn't broken. The brands are. Most are posting without purpose, reacting without strategy, creating without direction. I'm the one who changes that from the inside. Building strategies that stick. Turning data into decisions. Making brands impossible to ignore.

WHO?

I make brands
worth following.

I'm María Hernández. I work at the intersection of data and creativity because one without the other is just noise. From managing the social strategy of a multinational to co-founding a branding project from scratch, I've learned that the best content doesn't feel like content. It feels like something worth stopping for.

Type of project:

Social Media Strategy

Objective:

Brand Awareness

Prosegur is one of the world’s leading private security companies — present in 36 countries across 5 continents, with more than 180,000 people behind every operation. When a company this size turns 50, it doesn’t just celebrate. It redefines itself. My job was to make that shift visible on social — and make every post feel like culture, not just communication.

Insight

Most brands spend years searching for their big idea. Prosegur had one written in the name all along. PRO — the word that precedes every leader, every pioneer, every step forward. The campaign didn’t invent a concept. It named what Prosegur had been for five decades: 50 years being PRO.

PRO then.
PRO now.

Each carousel pairs two images — one from the past, one from today. The first shows what being PRO in people, innovation or security looked like 50 years ago. The second shows what it looks like now. No explanation needed. The contrast does the work.

PROSEGUR  →  CASH IS COOL  →  PROSEGUR  →  CASH IS COOL  →  PROSEGUR  →  CASH IS COOL  →  PROSEGUR  →  CASH IS COOL  →  PROSEGUR  →  CASH IS COOL  →  PROSEGUR  →  CASH IS COOL  →  PROSEGUR  →  CASH IS COOL  →  PROSEGUR  →  CASH IS COOL  →  PROSEGUR  →  CASH IS COOL  →  PROSEGUR  →  CASH IS COOL  → 

Type of project:

Social Media Strategy

Objective:

Make cash feel relevant to a generation
that pays with their phone

Prosegur moves 10% of the world’s cash. For a company of that scale, launching a social media campaign targeting a younger audience wasn’t just a content challenge — it was a repositioning challenge. Cash is Cool was Prosegur’s bold bet: turn a product everyone uses but nobody talks about into something worth following.

Insight

The brief wasn’t to defend cash. It was to reframe it. Stop competing with digital payments on utility — and start winning on identity, privacy and autonomy. The generation that pays with their phone doesn’t hate cash. They just never saw it as cool.

BRICKZ  →  REAL STATE  →  BRICKZ  →  REAL STATE  →  BRICKZ  →  REAL STATE  →  BRICKZ  →  REAL STATE  →  BRICKZ  →  REAL STATE  →  BRICKZ  →  REAL STATE  →  BRICKZ  →  REAL STATE  →  BRICKZ  →  REAL STATE  →  BRICKZ  →  REAL STATE  →  BRICKZ  →  REAL STATE  → 

Type of project:

Rebranding

Objective:

Reposition the brand as a more
sophisticated player in real estate

Brickz is a real estate newsletter built to make one of the most complex and volatile industries actually understandable. Not just for professionals — for anyone trying to make sense of it. When the founder came to us, the brand already existed. The problem wasn’t the idea. It was the image.

Insight

The audience is young — the “Z” in the name says it all. But the subject matter is serious. That tension was the whole brief: build an identity credible enough to be trusted, but not so formal it loses the generation it was made for. Sophisticated, not stiff.

The concept

Four buildings, one letter. The ‘i’ in BRICKZ, rotated and mirrored, becomes a city block seen from above. Navy for the glass, orange for the brick, white for the page it lives on.

GERO PALACIOS  →  GOLF  →  GERO PALACIOS  →  GOLF  →  GERO PALACIOS  →  GOLF  →  GERO PALACIOS  →  GOLF  →  GERO PALACIOS  →  GOLF  →  GERO PALACIOS  →  GOLF  →  GERO PALACIOS  →  GOLF  →  GERO PALACIOS  →  GOLF  →  GERO PALACIOS  →  GOLF  →  GERO PALACIOS  →  GOLF  → 

Type of project:

Personal Branding

Objective:

Make a golfer’s name worth remembering
— on and off the course

Gero Palacios is a professional golfer who doesn’t look like the cliché. No stiff collar, no velvet rope energy. He plays at national level, competes at the highest standard — and then goes home like anyone else. The challenge wasn’t to build a brand that screamed excellence. It was to build one that made excellence feel like something you could get close to. A mark for a player who is the best on the course and, the moment he steps off it, just one of us.

Insight

An eagle in golf means doing in two what takes others four. But the real insight wasn’t the score — it was the attitude behind it. No effort, no drama. Just someone who makes the impossible look like Tuesday. The brand was built around that energy: elite enough to command respect, relaxed enough to feel real. Eagle Territory became the space where the best don’t perform greatness — they just live it. And somehow, watching that, makes everyone feel like they could too.

The concept

The eagle wasn’t chosen for decoration. In golf, an eagle is the moment a player does in two what the course was designed to take four — precision, confidence, and a quiet refusal to follow the expected pace. The mascot takes that idea and makes it human: cap on, club in hand, expression that says it all without saying a word. Not a symbol of tradition — a symbol of attitude.

This is not finished.
What will we create next?
